mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes E200 / E230 petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned E200/E230 petrol engine typically costs £1,500–£5,500 supply only depending on generation. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A replacement M271.820 engine costs on average £3,094 in good condition. Main dealer new engines start from £9,000–£15,000+ supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes E200 / E230 petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which engine generation your E-Class has. The M111 (W210, 1996–2002) is bulletproof — robust and durable. The M271 (W211, 2002–2009) has timing chain and camshaft adjuster issues. The M272 V6 (2006–2009) has balance shaft and camshaft magnet oil leak problems. The M274 (2013–2020) has timing chain, thermostat and piston wrist pin issues. Check the Era Map above for your specific engine.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes E200 / E230 petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain and camshaft adjuster wear on M271 and M274 engines — causing cold-start rattle, balance shaft issues and camshaft magnet oil leaks on the M272 V6, thermostat and cooling system failures across all generations, and piston wrist pin issues on 2015–2016 M274 engines.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes E200 / E230 petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £4,000–£15,000 E-Class, a £2,500–£4,500 recon is 25–110% of the car's value. For a clean W210 M111 example, replacement is almost always worth it given the engine's legendary durability. For newer models with documented service history, replacement is viable. For examples under £3,000, the replacement cost may exceed our 70% Mercedes threshold.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes E200 / E230 petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: W210 (1996–2002) has M111, W211 (2002–2009) has M271 or M272, W212 (2009–2013) has M271, W212 facelift (2013–2016) and W213 (2016–2020) have M274, W213 facelift (2020–2023) has M264. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M271 camshaft adjuster, or does it need engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Camshaft adjuster replacement typically costs £800–£2,000 and is far cheaper than a catastrophic engine failure. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. The M271 is known for limited timing chain, sprocket and tensioner lifespan. If the chain jumps, a full engine replacement is usually the only option.

eraMap.title: Mercedes E200 / E230 Petrol — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 2.0L inline-four — 136 hp, 190 Nm. Robust and durable; basic maintenance keeps them running
2.3L inline-four — 150 hp, 220 Nm. Bulletproof M111 family
1.8L supercharged — 163 hp. Timing chain and camshaft adjuster issues are common
2.5L V6 — 204 hp. Balance shaft and camshaft magnet leaks documented
1.8L turbo — 184 hp. Camshaft adjuster and timing chain wear are key concerns
2.0L turbo — 184 hp. Timing chain sprocket wear and piston wrist pin issues reported
2.0L turbo — 181–197 hp. Timing chain issues and thermostat failures documented
2.0L turbo with 48V MHEV — 197 hp. Wiring and 48V system issues emerging

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes E200 / E230 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an E-Class petrol engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Timing chain and camshaft adjuster work on M271 and M274 engines requires specialist attention.

problems.title: Timing Chain & Camshaft Adjuster Wear (M271 & M274)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or grinding noise on cold start for 2–5 seconds, engine warning light, camshaft correlation fault codes (P0016, P0017), rough running, starting problems

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M271 and M274 engines are notorious for timing chain and camshaft adjuster (phaser) wear. One owner reported their E200 had "进气凸轮轴调节器故障" at just 49,000 km. When the camshaft adjuster fails, the timing chain can become slack and jump teeth, leading to piston-valve contact. At the first sign of cold-start rattling, have the camshaft adjusters and timing chain inspected immediately. A £800–£2,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£8,000 engine replacement. The M271 is particularly known for "малый ресурс ГРМ, цепи, звёзд и натяжителя".

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/m271-m274-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Balance Shaft & Camshaft Magnet Oil Leaks (M272 V6)
problems.symptoms: Check engine light, rough running, oil leaks from camshaft magnets, ECU failure due to oil contamination, timing chain wear
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,500 (balance shaft repair / camshaft magnet seal replacement) / £2,500–£5,000 (full timing chain kit including balance shaft)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you notice oil leaks or rough running

problems.recommendation: The M272 V6 has a well-documented balance shaft issue — the balance shaft gear wears prematurely, causing timing chain problems. Another known issue is the camshaft magnet oil leak, which can contaminate the ECU and wiring harness, leading to a £5,000 repair bill. "Plastic cylinder head plugs ooze oil" and the "entire timing drive becomes unusable". If you notice oil leaks from the camshaft magnets or timing chain noise, have the engine inspected immediately. Balance shaft replacement is a significant job requiring specialist attention.

failureLink.label: Read the full balance shaft failure guide → /failures/m272-balance-shaft/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Thermostat & Cooling System Failures (All Engines)
problems.symptoms: Engine running too cool (temperature stuck around 60–70°C), engine overheating, coolant loss, check engine light
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if temperature issues develop

problems.recommendation: The map-controlled thermostat is the "single most common failure" on the M272 and M273 engines. On M271 engines, a failed thermostat can cause the engine to run cool, with coolant temperatures stuck around 60–70°C. The M274 is also known for thermostat failure requiring significant disassembly. If you notice the temperature gauge not reaching normal operating temperature or erratic cooling, have the thermostat inspected. Catching this early prevents more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full cooling system failure guide

problems.title: Piston Wrist Pin & Cylinder Issues (M274, 2015–2016)
problems.symptoms: Knocking noise from the engine, misfires, oil consumption, loss of compression

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear knocking, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M274 engine from 2015–2016 is known for piston wrist pin failures — "Most of the M274 engine's that had the bad wrist pins where from 2015-2016". Some owners report "cracking Pistons". If you hear a knocking noise from the engine, have it inspected immediately. This is a serious issue that can lead to catastrophic engine failure. If you're considering a 2015–2016 E200 with the M274, a thorough pre-purchase inspection is essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full piston failure guide → /failures/m274-piston-wrist-pin/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: E200 / E230 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Full engine replacement is the only reliable fix
Catch early to prevent ECU damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This E200 / E230 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * W210 M111-engined car (1996–2002) — these are "bulletproof" and the most durable
* Documented timing chain / camshaft adjuster work on any M271 or M274 engine
* Full service history with regular oil changes using MB-approved oil
* M272 V6 with balance shaft work already completed
* No cold-start rattling — this is a critical warning sign
avoidIf: * M271 or M274-engined car with cold-start rattle and no timing chain history
* M272 V6 with no balance shaft or camshaft magnet leak history
* 2015–2016 M274 with no piston wrist pin inspection
* Poor service history — these engines are complex and need proper maintenance
* Check engine light with camshaft correlation codes

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The E200/E230 petrol badge spans five very different engine generations. The M111 (W210, 1996–2002) is a bulletproof engine — robust, durable and capable of very high mileages. The M271 (W211, 2002–2009) introduced timing chain and camshaft adjuster issues — the "малый ресурс ГРМ" is a genuine concern. The M272 V6 (2006–2009) has balance shaft and camshaft magnet oil leak problems. The M274 (2013–2020) has timing chain, thermostat and piston wrist pin issues (especially 2015–2016 models). The M264 adds 48V mild-hybrid complexity. Buy a W210 M111 if you want the most durable option. Buy a well-maintained later engine with documented service history. Buy on documented history, not on generation alone.

cta.label: → Compare E200 / E230 Petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes E200 or E230 petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or grinding noise for 2–5 seconds (camshaft adjuster / timing chain warning — this is critical)
* Service History — Including which engine era (M111, M271, M272, M274 or M264) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Camshaft Adjuster History — Has the timing chain or camshaft adjusters been replaced? (M271/M274 critical)
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil leaks from camshaft magnets (M272) and oil filter housing (M271)
* Thermostat Health — Check engine temperature reaches normal operating range; thermostat failures are common
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for P0016/P0017 (camshaft correlation) codes
* Piston Health — 2015–2016 M274 cars: any knocking? (Wrist pin issues)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

The M271 is known for "малый ресурс ГРМ, цепи, звёзд и натяжителя" — the timing chain, sprockets and tensioner have a limited lifespan.
